This suggests that the issue is not just about `InputTypeA` being null, but about the entire cost calculation being different when `RedPhiOp1R` or `OpR` is null.

Looking at the test output, it expects `icmp ult i64 [[WIDE_TRIP_COUNT]], 16` but gets `icmp ult i64 %wide.trip.count, 4`. This means the vectorization factor changed from 16 to 4. The number 4 is interesting - it might be a default or minimum vectorization factor when cost calculation fails or returns a very high cost.

The issue might be that when `RedPhiOp1R` is null, we're not setting `ExtAType` properly. Looking at the code, `ExtAType` is initialized to `TTI::PR_None`. If `RedPhiOp1R` is null, `ExtAType` remains `PR_None`. This might affect the cost calculation.

Also, `InputTypeB` might be null. Looking at the code, `InputTypeB` is only set in `HandleWiden`. If we don't call `HandleWiden`, `InputTypeB` remains null.

Actually, looking at the `HandleWiden` function (lines 328-342), it sets both `InputTypeA` and `InputTypeB`, as well as `ExtAType` and `ExtBType`. If we don't call `HandleWiden`, these remain at their initial values (null for types, `PR_None` for extend kinds).

So when `RedPhiOp1R` is null or not a `VPWidenCastRecipe` or `VPWidenRecipe`, we don't set any of these values properly. My fix only sets `InputTypeA`, but not `InputTypeB`, `ExtAType`, or `ExtBType`.
